data.narrative:
Xona Space Systems is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $281M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 5 private same-stage peers including Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace, and Loft Orbital.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $281M raised proxy, yielding $478M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of $1.1B across the 5 same-sector, same-stage private comparables, which range from $1.0B (Isar Aerospace, Loft Orbital) to $8.0B (Sierra Space). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales is not applicable given the absence of revenue disclosure for the target. The public sector median P/S of 42.9x cannot be deployed without disclosed financials.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $406M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $814M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation lift versus peer positioning. Key sensitivities include capital intensity and revenue trajectory relative to peers, which remain unspecified in available data.
